items.title: Upgrading TRAE Membership Benefits
items.summary: TRAE launches new token-based pricing with four membership tiers from $3 to $100 monthly, starting February 24, 2026. A 14-day free Pro trial for new users is also available after this transition.

items.title: Making AI Coding Safer: How TRAE Protects Your Codebase
items.summary: We're introducing sandboxing and a multi-layer security system designed to reduce security risk while keeping AI productive.

items.title: Quickstart to SOLO Coder
items.summary: This guide will get you started with using a coding agent like SOLO coder in just a few minutes. We'll show you how to use SOLO Coder for some common development tasks.

items.summary: TRAE SOLO is the responsive coding agent we built for real-world software development.

items.summary: How a lean team builds production features end-to-end with an AI engineer that “understands, executes, and delivers.”

items.title: Max Mode in TRAE
items.summary: We are introducing Max Mode, which is designed for deep coding with larger context windows and more tool calls.

items.title: Best Practices for TRAE Rules
items.summary: Stop repeating yourself. In this article we dive into how to set up TRAE Rules that remember your preferences, keep your team aligned, and avoid the common mistakes that make AI ignore your instructions.

items.title: Challenges and Considerations Behind Trae Cue
items.summary: Cue is one of the latest and most complex AI development tool features developed by TRAE. We explored the technical challenges, achievements as well as future plans for TRAE Cue.

items.title: Understanding Trae’s Data Practices
items.summary: This blog answers some of the most common questions about Trae’s data practices. We’ll explain where data is stored, how it’s used, and what options you have to control its collection and use.

items.title: Cue Major Update
items.summary: Trae Cue gets its biggest monthly update with major improvements to model performance and speed.

items.title: Fresh New Look
items.summary: TRAE 2.0 marks a full - scale design upgrade for a more focused, intelligent development experience.

items.title: SOLO is All You Need

items.title: Trae Agent 2.0: Smarter Architecture, Tools, and Memory
items.summary: Trae Agent 2.0 featured a reengineered architecture, unified agent modes, smarter tool orchestration, and long-term memory that brings us closer to building the real AI engineer.

items.title: @Agent: Rules + Tools to Define Your Future Agents
items.summary: Deep dive into @Agent and how we built it.

items.title: Collaborate with Intelligence
items.summary: Trae unveils major updates: a unified Chat - Builder interface, @Agent system with MCP for multi - agent collaboration, and smarter context handling (#Web, #Doc, Rules). Boost your workflow with AI - driven coding and adaptable agents.

items.title: Beyond AI Coding: Trae's Vision for Human-AI Collaboration
items.summary: Trae's vision is to empower seamless collaboration between humans and AI by advancing AI-driven coding, enabling developers to work smarter, faster, and more creatively.

items.title: More Agentic
items.summary: We've improved our agent capabilities with better context understanding and greater autonomy.

items.title: Trae Achieves #1 on SWE-bench Verified with Claude 3.7
items.summary: Trae achieves 71.0% accuracy on SWE-bench Verified with Claude 3.7, pushing the boundaries of automated issue resolving. Not just fast. Not just big. But deeply aligned with the nature of engineering.

items.title: Mastering Context in Trae
items.summary: We've put together this guide that shows you how to make smart use of context in Trae to collaborate seamlessly with your AI partner.

items.summary: This is your year in review. Let's look back at what we created together.
